summary: George Russell held off Max Verstappen's sustained pressure throughout the Austrian Grand Prix to secure Mercedes' victory, demonstrating the composure that had earned him an unexpected pole position through quick tactical thinking in qualifying. The tense battle between the two drivers tested Russell's nerve across the final laps as Red Bull mounted a late challenge, with the Mercedes driver ultimately executing the defensive driving needed to preserve his lead. Russell's second win of 2026 restores confidence ahead of his home race at Silverstone and tightens the championship picture as Mercedes pushes harder against Red Bull's challenge.
